# Mirodan / Laban — grounded findings (distilled, page-cited)

**Purpose:** the MD-first lookup so we don't re-read the 52MB PDF or the 65KB chapter extracts every time. Everything here has been verified against `mirodan-ch{1,2}-*.md` (vol 1 extracts) or `Mirodan-PhD-1997-Vol2.pdf`. Page refs are to the primary source. If you need something NOT here, see `mirodan-ch{1,2,3,4}-*.md`, then the PDF (per the `mirodan-grounding` skill).

Last updated 2026-06-04 (Weight re-root). Maintainers: append, don't silently rewrite — keep the citation log honest.

---

## 1. The four Motion Factors (Ch1)

| Factor | Poles | Inner Participation | Mental function |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Weight** | Strong ↔ Light | **Intending** | Sensing |
| **Space** | Direct ↔ Flexible | Attending | Thinking |
| **Time** | Sustained ↔ Quick | Deciding | Intuiting |
| **Flow** | Bound ↔ Free | Adapting | Feeling |

> "the words Weight, Space, Time and Flow have **no expressive power in themselves**" — Ch1 p.312. Never read a factor split from its Inner Participation root.

## 2. Weight — what it ACTUALLY signifies (LOAD-BEARING, re-rooted 2026-06-04)

Weight is **NOT "muscular force, nothing else."** That framing (our 2026-06-04 first pass) is **over-narrowed** — see `research/mirodan-weight-reroot-audit-2026-06-04.md`.

- **Weight = the visible trace of Intending** — "intentions, desires, needs, the **'will-power' to act** on given circumstances" (Ch1 p.281); "Intending — stimulating the mind purposefully" (Ch1 p.293). Expressed *physically* as muscular firmness / counter-pressure against gravity (Ch1 p.292, 316).
  > "Strong Intending — a sensory **muscular firmness**… from the **contraction of the central body muscles**." (Ch1 p.316)
- **Read Weight from will + exertion together.** Strong-Weight evidence = asserted will / resolve / desire pushing against resistance ("we will take it down", "this stops now", "I refuse"), OR literal force / mass / heavy landing. **Light** = buoyancy, fine touch, will withheld.
- **Epistemic certainty is NOT Weight.** "This is simply true / live / final / unhedged" is Space (**Direct**) + Awake (**certain**) — settled *awareness*, not asserted *will* (Ch1 p.298, 321; Ch2 p.481). Confidence about a *fact* ≠ a push of *will*.
- **Weight is NEVER a stress.** It is in every baseline's inner pair. A "weight-stressed" read means the stress is actually unknown.

### Commanding vs Certain — the discriminator (both are Direct)
> Does the line **EXERT will / impose a resolve** against resistance → **commanding** (Stable = Weight+Space, motor Pressing/Punching, p.494).
> Or **REGISTER a settled truth / awareness** → **certain** (Awake = Space+Time, p.481).
> Volitional pressure, not mere confidence, is the Weight signal.

Awake has **no Weight in its constitutive pair** (Space+Time) — but Weight is **latent, not absent**: the "certain" tempo's own motor is **Pressing**, a Strong action (Ch2 p.481). Never cite certainty as "proof of zero Weight."

## 3. The six Inner Attitudes (2-factor compounds, Ch2)

| Attitude | Factor pair | Baseline? | Gloss |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Stable** | Weight + Space | ✅ baseline | intelligent ruler, decisions taken (Werle, Logan Roy, the Duke) |
| **Near** | Weight + Time | ✅ baseline | down-to-earth, body-forward, relational (Big Daddy, the Nurse) |
| **Adream** | Weight + Flow | ✅ baseline | dreamy, yielding, feeling-led (Hamlet, Blanche, Ophelia) |
| **Mobile** | Time + Flow | ❌ action-only | fragmenting, scattered |
| **Awake** | Space + Time | ❌ action-only | alert sensor, strategist, noticing |
| **Remote** | Space + Flow | ❌ action-only | aristocratic-aloof, intellectual, withdrawn |

Only Stable / Near / Adream contain **Weight/Intending** → only they can be **baseline (resting) characters** (Ch2 p.474). Mobile / Awake / Remote are **Action Attitudes**: they appear as outer projections/flashes, never as a resting home.

## 4. Aspects (factor-led emphasis — available only when the attitude contains that factor)
- **Enclosing** — Weight-led (Intending). For stable, near, adream.
- **Penetrating** — Space-led (Attending). For stable, awake, remote.
- **Radiating** — Flow-led (Adapting). For adream, mobile, remote.
- **Circumscribing** — Time-led (Deciding). For near, mobile, awake.

## 5. Stresses (the activated 3rd factor) + legal stresses per baseline
Stress = the third Motion Factor added to a 2-factor attitude so it becomes visible action.
- **stable** → time OR flow (never space, never weight)
- **near** → space OR flow (never time, never weight)
- **adream** → time OR space (never flow, never weight)

If a sample seems to want an *illegal* stress, the gut you feel is the **Aspect**, not the stress (e.g. "space-stressed stable" = Penetrating Stable).

## 6. The 24 tempi (4 per attitude) — factor shape, motor, page

| Tempo | Attitude | Factor shape | Motor | Page |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| commanding | Stable | Strong + Direct | Pressing → Punching | p.494 |
| practical | Stable | Strong + Flexible | Wringing → Slashing | p.495 |
| self-contained | Stable | Light + Direct | Gliding → Dabbing | p.497 |
| receptive | Stable | Light + Flexible | Floating → Flicking | p.495 |
| materialistic | Near | Strong + Quick | Punching / Slashing | p.423 |
| human | Near | Light + Sustained | Floating / Gliding | p.426 |
| warm | Near | Strong + Sustained | Pressing / Wringing | p.427 |
| cool | Near | Light + Quick | Dabbing / Flicking | p.429 |
| sombre | Adream | Strong + Bound | Pressing → Punching | p.460 |
| overpowering | Adream | Strong + Free | Wringing → Slashing | p.465 |
| diffused | Adream | Light + Bound | Gliding → Dabbing | p.468 |
| irradiant | Adream | Light + Free | Floating → Flicking | p.462 |
| unacknowledged | Mobile (outer) | Quick + Bound | Punching / Slashing | p.514 |
| acknowledged | Mobile (outer) | Sustained + Free | Floating / Gliding | p.515 |
| revealed | Mobile (outer) | Quick + Free | Dabbing / Flicking | p.518 |
| concealed | Mobile (outer) | Sustained + Bound | Pressing / Wringing | p.520 |
| egocentric | Remote (outer) | Direct + Bound | Pressing → Punching | p.442 |
| unsociable | Remote (outer) | Flexible + Bound | Wringing → Slashing | p.447 |
| sociable | Remote (outer) | Direct + Free | Gliding → Dabbing | p.447 |
| altruistic | Remote (outer) | Flexible + Free | Floating → Flicking | p.444 |
| acute | Awake (outer) | Direct + Quick | Punching / Dabbing | p.480 |
| doubting | Awake (outer) | Flexible + Sustained | Floating / Wringing | p.481 |
| certain | Awake (outer) | Direct + Sustained | Pressing / Gliding | p.481 |
| uncertain | Awake (outer) | Flexible + Quick | Flicking / Slashing | p.483 |

(Canonical shorthand for each tempo lives in `src/actor-memory.ts` `MIRODAN_24_TEMPI` — that array is the machine-readable mirror of this table.)

## 7. The eight Working Actions (motor layer, Weight×Space×Time, Ch1 p.341)

| Working action | Weight | Space | Time |
|---|---|---|---|
| Pressing | Strong | Direct | Sustained |
| Punching / Thrusting | Strong | Direct | Quick |
| Wringing | Strong | Flexible | Sustained |
| Slashing | Strong | Flexible | Quick |
| Gliding | **Light** | Direct | Sustained |
| Dabbing | **Light** | Direct | Quick |
| Floating | **Light** | Flexible | Sustained |
| Flicking | **Light** | Flexible | Quick |

- **Motor must reconcile with the Weight pole.** Gliding/Dabbing/Floating/Flicking are Light; Wringing/Slashing/Pressing/Punching are Strong. Never emit a Strong tempo with a Light motor or vice versa.
- **Preparation hierarchy** (Ch1 p.347): a Quick action needs its Sustained prep first — Punching needs Pressing; Slashing needs Wringing; Dabbing needs Gliding; Flicking needs Floating. "We cannot carry out a Punching action without first undergoing the inner preparation of Pressing."

## 8. Drives (Ch3, brief)
A Drive is a three-factor activation named by the latent/subdued fourth factor:
- **Doing** (Weight+Space+Time; Flow latent), **Spell** (Weight+Space+Flow; Time latent), **Passion** (Weight+Time+Flow; Space latent), **Vision** (Space+Time+Flow; Weight latent).
- A drive/drive-axis is a **derived character-energy read**, not a transitive objective verb. The Actor performs verbs through Working Actions; the Director reads drive from the result.
- **Passion is Spaceless lost-Thinking emotion** (Ch3 p.534). Clock-urgency is Time/Quick-Deciding, NOT automatically Passion — don't equate urgency with Passion.

## 9. Known traps (cost us real debugging time)
1. **"Unhedged / confident = Strong Weight = commanding"** — CATEGORY ERROR. Certainty is Direct (Space); will is Weight. Fixed 2026-06-04 (this doc §2). Symptom when wrong: a classifier calls everything "commanding".
2. **Over-correction inverse** — "confidence is never Weight, require physical force" strips *asserted will* (which IS Weight) → everything collapses to "certain" and commanding falls to ~0%. The right axis is **will vs settledness**, not **force vs confidence**.
3. **Brevity = Strong** — no. A short hard line is Quick/Direct (a Dab) unless it carries will/weight-bearing build-up (then Punch, which needs a Pressing prep).
4. **Urgency = Passion** — no (see §8).
5. **Weight as a stress** — illegal (§2).

## 10. Citation integrity log (what's verbatim vs paraphrase)
- Ch1 p.281/293 (Intending = will-power to act): **verbatim**, load-bearing for §2.
- Ch1 p.292/316 (muscular force / central-muscle contraction): **verbatim**.
- Ch1 p.312 (factors have no expressive power alone): **verbatim**.
- Ch1 p.298 ("we often confuse Thinking/Attending with quick, direct, unhesitating decision taking"): **verbatim** but is a Thinking-vs-Time caution; supports "unhesitating ≠ Weight" by omission, mild stretch as a *direct* Weight proof.
- Ch1 p.321 (Direct = intentive/eager/penetrating; Flexible = tentative/hesitant): **verbatim**.
- Ch2 p.474 ("Awake… no Intending component… sensuously cold"): the word "Weightless" is **our coinage**, not Mirodan's — say "Weight non-constitutive / latent."
- Ch2 p.481 (certain = Direct/Sustained, motor Pressing/Gliding): **verbatim**; note Pressing = Strong, so certain's motor reaches into Strong-Weight.
- Tempo table (§6) page refs: mirror of `MIRODAN_24_TEMPI` in `src/actor-memory.ts`, sourced to Mirodan Vol. 2.
